metadata.name: slimevb
metadata.summary: Port of David Ha's Slime volleyball for gymnasium.

classes.name: slimevb.baseline_policy.BaselinePolicy

methods.name: slimevb.baseline_policy.BaselinePolicy.predict
documentation: take obs, update rnn state, return action
Tiny RNN policy with only 120 parameters of otoro.net/slimevolley agent

modules.name: slimevb.slimevolley_env
documentation:
Port of Neural Slime Volleyball to Python Gym Environment

David Ha (2020)

Original version:

https://otoro.net/slimevolley
https://blog.otoro.net/2015/03/28/neural-slime-volleyball/
https://github.com/hardmaru/neuralslimevolley

No dependencies apart from Numpy and Gym

classes.name: slimevb.slimevolley_env.SlimeVolleyEnv

methods.name: slimevb.slimevolley_env.SlimeVolleyEnv.__init__
documentation:
Reward modes:

net score = right agent wins minus left agent wins

0: returns net score (basic reward)
1: returns 0.01 x number of timesteps (max 3000) (survival reward)
2: sum of basic reward and survival reward

0 is suitable for evaluation, while 1 and 2 may be good for training

Setting multiagent to True puts in info (4th thing returned in stop)
the otherObs, the observation for the other agent. See multiagent.py

Setting self.from_pixels to True makes the observation with multiples
of 84, since usual atari wrappers downsample to 84x84

methods.name: slimevb.slimevolley_env.SlimeVolleyEnv.step
documentation:
note: although the action space is multi-binary, float vectors
are fine (refer to setAction() to see how they get interpreted)

Gym wrapper for Slime Volley game.

By default, the agent you are training controls the right agent
on the right. The agent on the left is controlled by the baseline
RNN policy.

Game ends when an agent loses 5 matches (or at t=3000 timesteps).

Note: Optional mode for MARL experiments, like self-play which
deviates from Gym env. Can be enabled via supplying optional action
to override the default baseline agent's policy:

obs1, reward, done, info = env.step(action1, action2)

the next obs for the right agent is returned in the optional
fourth item from the step() method.

reward is in the perspective of the right agent so the reward
for the left agent is the negative of this number.

functions.name: slimevb.core.utils.toX
documentation: conversion from space to pixels (allows us to render to diff resolutions)

classes.name: slimevb.core.utils.DelayScreen
documentation: initially the ball is held still for INIT_DELAY_FRAMES(30) frames

functions.name: slimevb.core.constants.setPixelObsMode
documentation:
used for experimental pixel-observation mode
note: new dim's chosen to be PIXEL_SCALE (2x) as Pixel Obs dims (will be downsampled)

also, both agent colors are identical, to potentially facilitate multiagent

variables.name: slimevb.core.constants.REF_U
documentation: ground height

variables.name: slimevb.core.constants.REF_WALL_WIDTH
documentation: wall width

variables.name: slimevb.core.constants.FRICTION
documentation: 1 means no FRICTION, less means FRICTION

variables.name: slimevb.core.constants.MAXLIVES
documentation: game ends when one agent loses this many games

variables.name: slimevb.core.constants.PIXEL_AGENT_LEFT_COLOR
documentation: AMBER

variables.name: slimevb.core.constants.PIXEL_AGENT_RIGHT_COLOR
documentation: AMBER

functions.name: slimevb.rendering.get_display
documentation:
Convert a display specification (such as :0) into an actual Display
object.

Pyglet only supports multiple Displays on Linux.

methods.name: slimevb.rendering.Viewer.draw_circle
documentation: Convenience

classes.name: slimevb.core.agent.Agent

methods.name: slimevb.core.agent.Agent.update_state
documentation: normalized to side, appears different for each agent's perspective
keeps track of the agent in the game. note this is not the policy network

classes.name: slime_volleyball.baseline_policy.BaselinePolicy

methods.name: slime_volleyball.baseline_policy.BaselinePolicy.predict
documentation: take obs, update rnn state, return action
Tiny RNN policy with only 120 parameters of otoro.net/slimevolley agent

functions.name: slimevb.core.objects.make_half_circle
documentation: helper function for pyglet renderer

functions.name: slimevb.core.objects._add_attrs
documentation: help scale the colors from 0-255 to 0.0-1.0 (pyglet renderer)

functions.name: slimevb.core.objects.rect
documentation: Processing style function to make it easy to port p5.js program to python

functions.name: slimevb.core.objects.half_circle
documentation: Processing style function to make it easy to port p5.js program to python

functions.name: slimevb.core.objects.circle
documentation: Processing style function to make it easy to port p5.js program to python

classes.name: slimevb.core.objects.Particle
documentation: used for the ball, and also for the round stub above the fence

classes.name: slimevb.core.objects.Wall
documentation: used for the fence, and also the ground

classes.name: slimevb.core.objects.RelativeState
documentation:
keeps track of the obs.
Note: the observation is from the perspective of the agent.
an agent playing either side of the fence must see obs the same way

classes.name: slimevb.core.game.SlimeVolleyGame

methods.name: slimevb.core.game.SlimeVolleyGame.step
documentation: main game loop

the main slime volley game.
can be used in various settings, such as ai vs ai, ai vs human, human vs human

documentation: conversion from space to pixels (allows us to render to diff resolutions)
